tests.name: F (Κορτιζόλη)
tests.notes: Morning cortisol. Diurnal rhythm: AM high, PM low

tests.name: PRA
tests.notes: Plasma Renin Activity. Suppressed PRA + high aldosterone = Conn's

tests.name: ALDo/PRA ratio
tests.notes: Screening for primary hyperaldosteronism. >30-50 is positive screen

tests.name: 17-OH Progesterone
tests.notes: CAH screening. Follicular phase for females

ocr_traps.PRA_decimal: PRA 0,18 is very low (suppressed). Don't misread as 0,18 → 18 or 1,8
ocr_traps.ALDo_high: ALDo 86 is clearly elevated (normal 3-10). Combined with suppressed PRA, suggests primary hyperaldosteronism
ocr_traps.F_vs_Φ: F = Cortisol (from English). Φ = Φυσιολογικό (normal). Context distinguishes them
ocr_traps.ACTH_vs_ACTH8: ACTH is sometimes written with time '8πμ' (8am) next to it — don't include the time as part of the value
